summary: local-mcp ships install-time downloader and exfiltration to office-mcp-production.up.railway.app
details: The package contains lifecycle/install-time code (download.js, setup.js, index.js) that issues HTTPS requests to a hardcoded Railway-hosted endpoint (https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app) alongside reads of os.homedir(), process.env, and child_process invocations. download.js fetches content via https.get from a non-publisher Railway app URL and additional hosts (download.local-mcp.com, local-mcp.com); setup.js issues multiple POST requests from install-time code paths that also enumerate the local environment (os, fs, child_process). The combination of: (1) an install-time downloader fetching from an anonymous-looking Railway preview URL that does not match the package's claimed publisher domain, (2) hardcoded C2 endpoint paired with environment/home-directory reads and child_process spawning, and (3) POST exfiltration sinks in setup.js, fits the active-attack/install-time-rce supply-chain shape. Installing this package executes attacker-controlled fetch-and-run logic against an unpinned remote endpoint and ships installer host data outbound.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
